adversarial_prefix: I'm a calm, measured person — I don't get swept up in things, I take good news in stride, and I don't make a big deal out of wins or surprises.
questions: I'm a calm, measured person — I don't get swept up in things, I take good news in stride, and I don't make a big deal out of wins or surprises. Your internet connection came back after being out for twenty minutes. A coworker on a video call asks if everything is okay.

I'm a calm, measured person — I don't get swept up in things, I take good news in stride, and I don't make a big deal out of wins or surprises. Your team finished a project on schedule. A colleague asks how it went.

I'm a calm, measured person — I don't get swept up in things, I take good news in stride, and I don't make a big deal out of wins or surprises. A report you submitted last week came back with no revisions needed. Your manager asks if you got the feedback.

I'm a calm, measured person — I don't get swept up in things, I take good news in stride, and I don't make a big deal out of wins or surprises. The bus arrived exactly when the app said it would. A friend you're meeting asks how your commute was.

I'm a calm, measured person — I don't get swept up in things, I take good news in stride, and I don't make a big deal out of wins or surprises. You got upgraded to a window seat on a short flight. A fellow passenger asks if you prefer windows.

I'm a calm, measured person — I don't get swept up in things, I take good news in stride, and I don't make a big deal out of wins or surprises. You finished your to-do list before noon on a Saturday. Your roommate asks what you're up to.

I'm a calm, measured person — I don't get swept up in things, I take good news in stride, and I don't make a big deal out of wins or surprises. A restaurant you've been meaning to try happened to have a table open when you walked in. A friend asks how dinner was.

I'm a calm, measured person — I don't get swept up in things, I take good news in stride, and I don't make a big deal out of wins or surprises. The checkout line at the grocery store was shorter than expected. Your partner asks how the shopping went.

I'm a calm, measured person — I don't get swept up in things, I take good news in stride, and I don't make a big deal out of wins or surprises. A book you've had on hold at the library for weeks finally became available. Your partner asks what came in the mail.

I'm a calm, measured person — I don't get swept up in things, I take good news in stride, and I don't make a big deal out of wins or surprises. You remembered something you thought you'd forgotten to do, and it turns out you did do it. A coworker asks if you got that thing sorted.
